bead.title: global: text-lever legacy CSS utility used in 5+ files — should be fully replaced by text-accent-lever
bead.description:
The CSS utility class .text-lever is defined in src/app.css line 190 as a legacy alias for --status-open-text (the open bead status color). It is used in at least 6 files as a shorthand for ID column coloring:

- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/+page.svelte (lines 7, 13)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (line 207)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte (line 367)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/beads/+layout.svelte (line 438)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte (line 382)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte (line 402)
- src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/beads/+page.svelte (line 247)

The intent is to style IDs with the accent-lever color. Using .text-lever instead of text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever means the ID color does not adapt to dark mode (it's always the light-mode status-open-text color), and it obscures the semantic intent.

The .text-lever utility in app.css should be deprecated. All usages should be replaced with text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever.
bead.acceptance: All text-lever usages removed from .svelte files. Replaced with text-accent-lever dark:text-dark-accent-lever. The .text-lever definition in app.css removed or marked deprecated. Dark mode ID columns now correctly use dark-accent-lever.
